steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank separates two independent clauses: 'the observatory sites... number nearly five [BLANK]' and 'the radars track mesospheric winds and other geospace phenomena.' Two independent clauses require a period, semicolon, or coordinating conjunction — not a comma alone.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'dozen, located across the globe,': places only a comma between the two independent clauses — a comma splice. ✗
  • Choice B — 'dozen. Located across the globe,': uses a period to end the first sentence, then opens the second with the participial phrase ‘Located across the globe,’ which correctly modifies ‘the radars.’ Grammatically sound. ✓
  • Choice C — 'dozen, and located across the globe,': the coordinating conjunction ‘and’ here joins a modifier, not a full clause, leaving a comma splice between the two independent clauses. ✗
  • Choice D — 'dozen, which are located across the globe,': inserts a relative clause but still connects the two independent clauses with only a comma — another comma splice. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
